content: 怎么处理中断与恢复
我来帮你了解 Eino 框架中如何处理 Agent 的中断与恢复机制。让我先加载相关的技能指导。

1	Human-in-the-loop (HITL) enables agents to pause execution, request human input, and resume from where they stopped.
     2	
     3	## Core Concepts
     4	
     5	1. **Interrupt**: Agent pauses and sends info to the user (e.g., "approve this action?")
     6	2. **Checkpoint**: Framework saves execution state to a CheckPointStore
     7	3. **Resume**: User provides input, framework restores state and continues
     8	
     9	## Quick Start: Approval Pattern
    10	
    11	### 1. Create a tool that can interrupt
    12	
    13	```go
    14	import (
    15	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/adk"
    16	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/components/tool"
    17	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/components/tool/utils"
    18	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/compose"
    19	)
    20	
    21	type bookInput struct {
    22	    Location      string `json:"location"`
    23	    PassengerName string `json:"passenger_name"`
    24	}
    25	
    26	bookTool, _ := utils.InferTool("BookTicket", "Book a ticket",
    27	    func(ctx context.Context, input *bookInput) (string, error) {
    28	        return "success", nil
    29	    })
    30	
    31	// Wrap with approval -- interrupts before execution
    32	approvalTool := &tool.InvokableApprovableTool{InvokableTool: bookTool}
    33	```
    34	
    35	### 2. Create agent and runner with CheckPointStore
    36	
    37	```go
    38	agent, _ := adk.NewChatModelAgent(ctx, &adk.ChatModelAgentConfig{
    39	    Name:        "TicketBooker",
    40	    Instruction: "Book tickets using the BookTicket tool.",
    41	    Model:       cm,
    42	    ToolsConfig: adk.ToolsConfig{
    43	        ToolsNodeConfig: compose.ToolsNodeConfig{
    44	            Tools: []tool.BaseTool{approvalTool},
    45	        },
    46	    },
    47	})
    48	
    49	runner := adk.NewRunner(ctx, adk.RunnerConfig{
    50	    Agent:           agent,
    51	    EnableStreaming:  true,
    52	    CheckPointStore: store.NewInMemoryStore(), // Use Redis in production
    53	})
    54	```
    55	
    56	### 3. Run and handle interrupt
    57	
    58	```go
    59	iter := runner.Query(ctx, "Book a ticket to Beijing for Martin", adk.WithCheckPointID("session-1"))
    60	
    61	var interruptID string
    62	for {
    63	    event, ok := iter.Next()
    64	    if !ok {
    65	        break
    66	    }
    67	    if event.Err != nil {
    68	        log.Fatal(event.Err)
    69	    }
    70	    if event.Action != nil && event.Action.Interrupted != nil {
    71	        // Save the interrupt ID for resuming later
    72	        interruptID = event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts[0].ID
    73	        fmt.Printf("Interrupt: %v\n", event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts[0].Info)
    74	        break
    75	    }
    76	    // Handle normal output...
    77	}
    78	```
    79	
    80	### 4. Resume with user decision
    81	
    82	```go
    83	// User approves
    84	apResult := &tool.ApprovalResult{Approved: true}
    85	
    86	iter, err := runner.ResumeWithParams(ctx, "session-1", &adk.ResumeParams{
    87	    Targets: map[string]any{
    88	        interruptID: apResult,
    89	    },
    90	})
    91	if err != nil {
    92	    log.Fatal(err)
    93	}
    94	
    95	// Continue processing events
    96	for {
    97	    event, ok := iter.Next()
    98	    if !ok {
    99	        break
   100	    }
   101	    // Handle events...
   102	}
   103	```
   104	
   105	## Interrupt APIs (ADK Layer)
   106	
   107	### Simple Interrupt
   108	
   109	```go
   110	// In a custom agent's Run method:
   111	return adk.Interrupt(ctx, "Please clarify your request.")
   112	```
   113	
   114	### Stateful Interrupt
   115	
   116	```go
   117	// Save internal state for later restoration
   118	state := &MyState{ProcessedItems: 42}
   119	return adk.StatefulInterrupt(ctx, "Need user feedback", state)
   120	```
   121	
   122	### Composite Interrupt (for multi-agent)
   123	
   124	```go
   125	// When a parent agent's sub-agent interrupts
   126	return adk.CompositeInterrupt(ctx, "Sub-agent needs attention", parentState, subInterruptSignals...)
   127	```
   128	
   129	## Interrupt APIs (Compose Layer -- for tools/graph nodes)
   130	
   131	```go
   132	import "github.com/cloudwego/eino/compose"
   133	
   134	// Simple interrupt from a tool
   135	return "", compose.Interrupt(ctx, "Waiting for approval")
   136	
   137	// Stateful interrupt
   138	return "", compose.StatefulInterrupt(ctx, "Need input", myState)
   139	```
   140	
   141	## InterruptInfo Structure
   142	
   143	When an interrupt occurs, the event contains structured info:
   144	
   145	```go
   146	if event.Action != nil && event.Action.Interrupted != nil {
   147	    for _, point := range event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts {
   148	        fmt.Printf("ID: %s\n", point.ID)           // Unique interrupt address
   149	        fmt.Printf("Info: %v\n", point.Info)         // User-facing info
   150	        fmt.Printf("Root cause: %v\n", point.IsRootCause)
   151	    }
   152	}
   153	```
   154	
   155	## Resume APIs
   156	
   157	### ResumeWithParams (recommended)
   158	
   159	```go
   160	iter, err := runner.ResumeWithParams(ctx, checkpointID, &adk.ResumeParams{
   161	    Targets: map[string]any{
   162	        interruptID1: userData1,
   163	        interruptID2: userData2,
   164	    },
   165	})
   166	```
   167	
   168	### Legacy Resume (with tool options)
   169	
   170	```go
   171	iter, err := runner.Resume(ctx, checkpointID,
   172	    adk.WithToolOptions([]tool.Option{WithNewInput("user response")}),
   173	)
   174	```
   175	
   176	## ResumableAgent Interface
   177	
   178	Agents that support interrupt/resume must implement:
   179	
   180	```go
   181	type ResumableAgent interface {
   182	    Agent
   183	    Resume(ctx context.Context, info *ResumeInfo, opts ...AgentRunOption) *AsyncIterator[*AgentEvent]
   184	}
   185	
   186	type ResumeInfo struct {
   187	    WasInterrupted bool   // Always true when Resume is called
   188	    InterruptState any    // State saved via StatefulInterrupt
   189	    IsResumeTarget bool   // Whether this agent is the explicit resume target
   190	    ResumeData     any    // User-provided data for this agent
   191	}
   192	```
   193	
   194	ChatModelAgent implements ResumableAgent by default.
   195	
   196	## CheckPointStore
   197	
   198	```go
   199	type CheckPointStore interface {
   200	    Set(ctx context.Context, key string, value []byte) error
   201	    Get(ctx context.Context, key string) ([]byte, bool, error)
   202	}
   203	```
   204	
   205	Built-in: `store.NewInMemoryStore()` (for development). Use Redis or similar for production.
   206	
   207	State serialization uses `encoding/gob`. Register custom types:
   208	
   209	```go
   210	func init() {
   211	    gob.RegisterName("mypackage.MyType", &MyType{})
   212	}
   213	```
   214	
   215	## Common HITL Patterns
   216	
   217	| Pattern | Description | Example |
   218	|---------|-------------|---------|
   219	| Approval | Pause before executing an action | Tool execution approval |
   220	| Review & Edit | Let user modify tool arguments | Edit API call parameters |
   221	| Feedback Loop | Iterative refinement with human feedback | Content generation review |
   222	| Follow-up | Agent asks for clarification | Missing information prompts |
   223	
   224	## Complete Clarification Example
   225	
   226	```go
   227	// Tool that asks user for clarification
   228	type askInput struct {
   229	    Question string `json:"question" jsonschema_description:"Question to ask the user"`
   230	}
   231	
   232	askTool, _ := utils.InferTool("ask_user", "Ask user for clarification",
   233	    func(ctx context.Context, input *askInput) (string, error) {
   234	        // Interrupt to ask user
   235	        return "", compose.Interrupt(ctx, input.Question)
   236	    })
   237	
   238	// ... configure agent with askTool ...
   239	
   240	// Handle interrupt
   241	iter := runner.Query(ctx, "Recommend me some books", adk.WithCheckPointID("session-1"))
   242	var interruptID string
   243	for {
   244	    event, ok := iter.Next()
   245	    if !ok {
   246	        break
   247	    }
   248	    if event.Action != nil && event.Action.Interrupted != nil {
   249	        interruptID = event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts[0].ID
   250	        fmt.Printf("Agent asks: %v\n", event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts[0].Info)
   251	        break
   252	    }
   253	}
   254	
   255	// Resume with user's answer
   256	iter, _ = runner.ResumeWithParams(ctx, "session-1", &adk.ResumeParams{
   257	    Targets: map[string]any{
   258	        interruptID: "I want fiction books",
   259	    },
   260	})
   261	```

    14	import (
    15	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/adk"
    16	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/components/tool"

   105	## Interrupt APIs (ADK Layer)

   181	type ResumableAgent interface {

   196	## CheckPointStore

   108

   124	```go
   125	// When a parent agent's sub-agent interrupts
   126	return adk.CompositeInterrupt(ctx, "Sub-agent needs attention", parentState, subInterruptSignals...)
   127	```
   128	
   129	## Interrupt APIs (Compose Layer -- for tools/graph nodes)
   130	
   131	```go
   132	import "github.com/cloudwego/eino/compose"
   133	
   134	// Simple interrupt from a tool
   135	return "", compose.Interrupt(ctx, "Waiting for approval")
   136	
   137	// Stateful interrupt
   138	return "", compose.StatefulInterrupt(ctx, "Need input", myState)
   139	```
   140	
   141	## InterruptInfo Structure
   142	
   143	When an interrupt occurs, the event contains structured info:
   144	
   145	```go
   146	if event.Action != nil && event.Action.Interrupted != nil {
   147	    for _, point := range event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts {
   148	        fmt.Printf("ID: %s\n", point.ID)           // Unique interrupt address
   149	        fmt.Printf("Info: %v\n", point.Info)         // User-facing info
   150	        fmt.Printf("Root cause: %v\n", point.IsRootCause)
   151	    }
   152	}
   153	```
   154	
   155	## Resume APIs
   156	
   157	### ResumeWithParams (recommended)
   158	
   159	```go
   160	iter, err := runner.ResumeWithParams(ctx, checkpointID, &adk.ResumeParams{
   161	    Targets: map[string]any{
   162	        interruptID1: userData1,
   163	        interruptID2: userData2,
   164	    },
   165	})
   166	```
   167	
   168	### Legacy Resume (with tool options)
   169	
   170	```go
   171	iter, err := runner.Resume(ctx, checkpointID,
   172	    adk.WithToolOptions([]tool.Option{WithNewInput("user response")}),
   173	)
   174	```
   175	
   176	## ResumableAgent Interface
   177	
   178	Agents that support interrupt/resume must implement:
   179	
   180	```go
   181	type ResumableAgent interface {
   182	    Agent
   183	    Resume(ctx context.Context, info *ResumeInfo, opts ...AgentRunOption) *AsyncIterator[*AgentEvent]
   184	}
   185	
   186	type ResumeInfo struct {
   187	    WasInterrupted bool   // Always true when Resume is called
   188	    InterruptState any    // State saved via StatefulInterrupt
   189	    IsResumeTarget bool   // Whether this agent is the explicit resume target
   190	    ResumeData     any    // User-provided data for this agent
   191	}
   192	```
   193	
   194	ChatModelAgent implements ResumableAgent by default.
   195	
   196	## CheckPointStore
   197	
   198	```go
   199	type CheckPointStore interface {
   200	    Set(ctx context.Context, key string, value []byte) error
   201	    Get(ctx context.Context, key string) ([]byte, bool, error)
   202	}
   203	```
   204	
   205	Built-in: `store.NewInMemoryStore()` (for development). Use Redis or similar for production.
   206	
   207	State serialization uses `encoding/gob`. Register custom types:
   208	
   209	```go
   210	func init() {
   211	    gob.RegisterName("mypackage.MyType", &MyType{})
   212	}
   213	```
   214	
   215	## Common HITL Patterns
   216	
   217	| Pattern | Description | Example |
   218	|---------|-------------|---------|
   219	| Approval | Pause before executing an action | Tool execution approval |
   220	| Review & Edit | Let user modify tool arguments | Edit API call parameters |
   221	| Feedback Loop | Iterative refinement with human feedback | Content generation review |
   222	| Follow-up | Agent asks for clarification | Missing information prompts |
   223	
   224	## Complete Clarification Example
   225	
   226	```go
   227	// Tool that asks user for clarification
   228	type askInput struct {
   229	    Question string `json:"question" jsonschema_description:"Question to ask the user"`
   230	}
   231	
   232	askTool, _ := utils.InferTool("ask_user", "Ask user for clarification",
   233	    func(ctx context.Context, input *askInput) (string, error) {
   234	        // Interrupt to ask user
   235	        return "", compose.Interrupt(ctx, input.Question)
   236	    })
   237	
   238	// ... configure agent with askTool ...
   239	
   240	// Handle interrupt
   241	iter := runner.Query(ctx, "Recommend me some books", adk.WithCheckPointID("session-1"))
   242	var interruptID string
   243	for {
   244	    event, ok := iter.Next()
   245	    if !ok {
   246	        break
   247	    }
   248	    if event.Action != nil && event.Action.Interrupted != nil {
   249	        interruptID = event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts[0].ID
   250	        fmt.Printf("Agent asks: %v\n", event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts[0].Info)
   251	        break
   252	    }
   253	}
   254	
   255	// Resume with user's answer
   256	iter, _ = runner.ResumeWithParams(ctx, "session-1", &adk.ResumeParams{
   257	    Targets: map[string]any{
   258	        interruptID: "I want fiction books",
   259	    },
   260	})
   261	```

1	DeepAgent is a pre-built agent on top of ChatModelAgent that provides planning, filesystem access, shell execution, and sub-agent delegation out of the box.
     2	
     3	## Import
     4	
     5	Requires eino >= v0.5.14:
     6	
     7	```go
     8	import "github.com/cloudwego/eino/adk/prebuilt/deep"
     9	```
    10	
    11	## When to Use
    12	
    13	Use DeepAgent instead of plain ChatModelAgent when you need:
    14	
    15	- Built-in task planning (WriteTodos tool)
    16	- File system operations (read/write/edit/glob/grep)
    17	- Shell command execution
    18	- Sub-agent delegation with context isolation
    19	- Auto-summarization for long conversations
    20	
    21	Use plain ChatModelAgent when:
    22	
    23	- You need fine-grained control over tools and prompts
    24	- The task is simple (single tool, no planning needed)
    25	- You want to minimize token cost (DeepAgent's planning adds overhead)
    26	
    27	## Architecture
    28	
    29	```
    30	MainAgent (ChatModelAgent + built-in tools + prompt)
    31	    |
    32	    +-- WriteTodos tool (task planning)
    33	    +-- Built-in tools (read_file, write_file, edit_file, glob, grep, execute)
    34	    +-- TaskTool -> SubAgents
    35	            |
    36	            +-- GeneralPurpose (same tools as main, no TaskTool)
    37	            +-- Custom SubAgents
    38	```
    39	
    40	- MainAgent receives user input, plans via WriteTodos, delegates via TaskTool
    41	- SubAgents have isolated context (no shared history with MainAgent)
    42	- GeneralPurpose sub-agent is added by default for generic tasks
    43	
    44	## Configuration
    45	
    46	```go
    47	type Config struct {
    48	    // Required: the LLM model
    49	    Model model.ToolCallingChatModel
    50	
    51	    // Optional: filesystem backend for file operations
    52	    Backend filesystem.Backend
    53	
    54	    // Optional: shell execution (mutually exclusive with StreamingShell)
    55	    Shell filesystem.Shell
    56	
    57	    // Optional: streaming shell execution
    58	    StreamingShell filesystem.StreamingShell
    59	
    60	    // Optional: custom tools added to the main agent
    61	    Tools []tool.BaseTool
    62	
    63	    // Optional: custom sub-agents
    64	    SubAgents []adk.Agent
    65	
    66	    // Optional: custom system prompt (appended to built-in prompt)
    67	    Instruction string
    68	
    69	    // Optional: disable WriteTodos tool
    70	    WithoutWriteTodos bool
    71	
    72	    // Optional: disable the default general-purpose sub-agent
    73	    WithoutGeneralSubAgent bool
    74	
    75	    // Optional: custom TaskTool description generator
    76	    TaskToolDescriptionGenerator func(ctx context.Context, agents []adk.Agent) string
    77	
    78	    // Optional: middleware
    79	    Handlers []adk.ChatModelAgentMiddleware
    80	}
    81	```
    82	
    83	## Quick Start
    84	
    85	```go
    86	import (
    87	    "context"
    88	    "fmt"
    89	    "log"
    90	
    91	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino-ext/components/model/openai"
    92	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/adk"
    93	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/adk/filesystem"
    94	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/adk/prebuilt/deep"
    95	)
    96	
    97	func main() {
    98	    ctx := context.Background()
    99	
   100	    cm, _ := openai.NewChatModel(ctx, &openai.ChatModelConfig{
   101	        APIKey: "your-key", Model: "gpt-4o",
   102	    })
   103	
   104	    backend := filesystem.NewInMemoryBackend()
   105	
   106	    agent, err := deep.New(ctx, &deep.Config{
   107	        Model:   cm,
   108	        Backend: backend,
   109	    })
   110	    if err != nil {
   111	        log.Fatal(err)
   112	    }
   113	
   114	    runner := adk.NewRunner(ctx, adk.RunnerConfig{Agent: agent})
   115	    iter := runner.Query(ctx, "Analyze the CSV file at /data/sales.csv and create a summary report")
   116	
   117	    for {
   118	        event, ok := iter.Next()
   119	        if !ok {
   120	            break
   121	        }
   122	        if event.Err != nil {
   123	            log.Fatal(event.Err)
   124	        }
   125	        if event.Output != nil && event.Output.MessageOutput != nil {
   126	            msg, _ := event.Output.MessageOutput.GetMessage()
   127	            fmt.Printf("[%s] %s\n", event.AgentName, msg.Content)
   128	        }
   129	    }
   130	}
   131	```
   132	
   133	## Built-in Capabilities
   134	
   135	### WriteTodos
   136	
   137	A planning tool that lets the agent create and track a structured task list. The agent calls WriteTodos to decompose complex tasks, then updates progress as it works.
   138	
   139	Disable with `WithoutWriteTodos: true`.
   140	
   141	### File System Tools
   142	
   143	When `Backend` is configured, the agent gets: `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `glob`, `grep`.
   144	
   145	When `Shell` or `StreamingShell` is configured, the agent also gets: `execute`.
   146	
   147	### Task Delegation (TaskTool)
   148	
   149	When `SubAgents` are configured (or the default general-purpose sub-agent is enabled), the agent gets a `task` tool. The agent specifies which sub-agent to call and what task to perform.
   150	
   151	Sub-agents run in isolated context -- they only receive the task description, not the full conversation history.
   152	
   153	## Comparison with Other Patterns
   154	
   155	| Feature | DeepAgent | Plain ReAct | Plan-Execute |
   156	|---------|-----------|-------------|--------------|
   157	| Planning | Built-in (WriteTodos) | Manual | Separate planner agent |
   158	| Context isolation | Yes (sub-agents) | No | Depends |
   159	| Token cost | Higher (planning overhead) | Lower | Higher (separate plan) |
   160	| Model requirements | Higher (must plan well) | Lower | Medium |

1	ChatModelAgent is the core ADK agent that uses the ReAct pattern: LLM reasons, generates tool calls, executes tools, feeds results back, and loops until done.
     2	
     3	## Import
     4	
     5	```go
     6	import "github.com/cloudwego/eino/adk"
     7	```
     8	
     9	## ReAct Pattern
    10	
    11	1. Call ChatModel (Reason)
    12	2. LLM returns tool call requests (Action)
    13	3. ChatModelAgent executes tools (Act)
    14	4. Tool results fed back to ChatModel (Observation)
    15	5. Loop until ChatModel decides no more tool calls are needed
    16	
    17	When no tools are configured, ChatModelAgent degrades to a single ChatModel call.
    18	
    19	## ChatModelAgentConfig
    20	
    21	```go
    22	type ChatModelAgentConfig struct {
    23	    // Name of the agent. Should be unique across all agents.
    24	    Name string
    25	
    26	    // Description of capabilities. Helps other agents decide whether to delegate.
    27	    Description string
    28	
    29	    // Instruction used as system prompt. Supports f-string placeholders for session values:
    30	    // "The current time is {Time}. The user is {User}."
    31	    Instruction string
    32	
    33	    // The LLM model. Must support tool calling.
    34	    Model model.ToolCallingChatModel
    35	
    36	    // Tool configuration
    37	    ToolsConfig ToolsConfig
    38	
    39	    // Custom function to transform instruction + input into model messages.
    40	    // Optional. Defaults to prepending instruction as system message.
    41	    GenModelInput GenModelInput
    42	
    43	    // Exit tool. When called, agent terminates immediately.
    44	    // Use the built-in ExitTool: adk.ExitTool{}
    45	    Exit tool.BaseTool
    46	
    47	    // Max ReAct iterations. Default: 20. Agent errors if exceeded.
    48	    MaxIterations int
    49	
    50	    // Retry config for ChatModel failures. Optional.
    51	    ModelRetryConfig *ModelRetryConfig
    52	
    53	    // Middleware list
    54	    Handlers []ChatModelAgentMiddleware
    55	}
    56	```
    57	
    58	## ToolsConfig
    59	
    60	```go
    61	type ToolsConfig struct {
    62	    compose.ToolsNodeConfig
    63	
    64	    // Tools whose results cause the agent to return immediately (skip further ReAct loops).
    65	    ReturnDirectly map[string]bool
    66	
    67	    // When true, internal events from AgentTool sub-agents are emitted to the parent.
    68	    EmitInternalEvents bool
    69	}
    70	```
    71	
    72	`ToolsNodeConfig` comes from `github.com/cloudwego/eino/compose`:
    73	
    74	```go
    75	type ToolsNodeConfig struct {
    76	    Tools              []tool.BaseTool
    77	    ToolCallMiddlewares []compose.ToolCallMiddleware
    78	}
    79	```
    80	
    81	## Creating Tools
    82	
    83	Use `utils.InferTool` for quick tool creation:
    84	
    85	```go
    86	import (
    87	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/components/tool/utils"
    88	)
    89	
    90	type SearchInput struct {
    91	    Query string `json:"query" jsonschema_description:"Search query"`
    92	}
    93	
    94	type SearchOutput struct {
    95	    Results []string `json:"results"`
    96	}
    97	
    98	searchTool, err := utils.InferTool("web_search", "Search the web",
    99	    func(ctx context.Context, input *SearchInput) (*SearchOutput, error) {
   100	        return &SearchOutput{Results: []string{"result1"}}, nil
   101	    })
   102	```
   103	
   104	For tools that accept options (needed for interrupt/resume):
   105	
   106	```go
   107	optionableTool, err := utils.InferOptionableTool("ask_user", "Ask user for input",
   108	    func(ctx context.Context, input *AskInput, opts ...tool.Option) (string, error) {
   109	        o := tool.GetImplSpecificOptions[myOptions](nil, opts...)
   110	        if o.NewInput == nil {
   111	            return "", compose.NewInterruptAndRerunErr(input.Question)
   112	        }
   113	        return *o.NewInput, nil
   114	    })
   115	```
   116	
   117	## Complete Example with Streaming
   118	
   119	```go
   120	import (
   121	    "context"
   122	    "fmt"
   123	    "io"
   124	    "log"
   125	
   126	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino-ext/components/model/openai"
   127	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/adk"
   128	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/components/tool"
   129	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/components/tool/utils"
   130	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/compose"
   131	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/schema"
   132	)
   133	
   134	func main() {
   135	    ctx := context.Background()
   136	
   137	    // Create model
   138	    cm, _ := openai.NewChatModel(ctx, &openai.ChatModelConfig{
   139	        APIKey: "your-key", Model: "gpt-4o",
   140	    })
   141	
   142	    // Create tools
   143	    weatherTool, _ := utils.InferTool("get_weather", "Get weather for a city",
   144	        func(ctx context.Context, input *struct {
   145	            City string `json:"city" jsonschema_description:"City name"`
   146	        }) (string, error) {
   147	            return fmt.Sprintf("25C in %s", input.City), nil
   148	        })
   149	
   150	    calcTool, _ := utils.InferTool("calculator", "Basic math operations",
   151	        func(ctx context.Context, input *struct {
   152	            Expression string `json:"expression" jsonschema_description:"Math expression"`
   153	        }) (string, error) {
   154	            return "42", nil
   155	        })
   156	
   157	    // Create agent
   158	    agent, _ := adk.NewChatModelAgent(ctx, &adk.ChatModelAgentConfig{
   159	        Name:        "Assistant",
   160	        Description: "A helpful assistant with weather and calculator tools",
   161	        Instruction: "You are a helpful assistant. Use tools when needed.",
   162	        Model:       cm,
   163	        ToolsConfig: adk.ToolsConfig{
   164	            ToolsNodeConfig: compose.ToolsNodeConfig{
   165	                Tools: []tool.BaseTool{weatherTool, calcTool},
   166	            },
   167	        },
   168	        MaxIterations: 10,
   169	    })
   170	
   171	    // Run with streaming
   172	    runner := adk.NewRunner(ctx, adk.RunnerConfig{
   173	        Agent:          agent,
   174	        EnableStreaming: true,
   175	    })
   176	
   177	    iter := runner.Query(ctx, "What's the weather in Tokyo?")
   178	    for {
   179	        event, ok := iter.Next()
   180	        if !ok {
   181	            break
   182	        }
   183	        if event.Err != nil {
   184	            log.Fatal(event.Err)
   185	        }
   186	
   187	        if event.Output != nil && event.Output.MessageOutput != nil {
   188	            mv := event.Output.MessageOutput
   189	            if mv.IsStreaming {
   190	                // Handle streaming response
   191	                for {
   192	                    msg, err := mv.MessageStream.Recv()
   193	                    if err == io.EOF {
   194	                        break
   195	                    }
   196	                    if err != nil {
   197	                        log.Fatal(err)
   198	                    }
   199	                    fmt.Print(msg.Content)
   200	                }
   201	                fmt.Println()
   202	            } else {
   203	                // Handle non-streaming response
   204	                fmt.Printf("[%s] %s\n", mv.Role, mv.Message.Content)
   205	            }
   206	        }
   207	    }
   208	}
   209	```
   210	
   211	## Middleware
   212	
   213	Add middleware via the `Handlers` field:
   214	
   215	```go
   216	agent, _ := adk.NewChatModelAgent(ctx, &adk.ChatModelAgentConfig{
   217	    // ...
   218	    Handlers: []adk.ChatModelAgentMiddleware{
   219	        patchToolCallsMW,
   220	        summarizationMW,
   221	        filesystemMW,
   222	    },
   223	})
   224	```
   225	
   226	## ModelRetryConfig
   227	
   228	Configure automatic retry on model failures:
   229	
   230	```go
   231	agent, _ := adk.NewChatModelAgent(ctx, &adk.ChatModelAgentConfig{
   232	    // ...
   233	    ModelRetryConfig: &adk.ModelRetryConfig{
   234	        MaxRetries: 3,
   235	        // Additional retry policy fields...
   236	    },
   237	})
   238	```
   239	
   240	When a streaming response fails and will be retried, the stream returns a `WillRetryError`. Handle it to show retry status to the user.
   241	
   242	## SessionValues
   243	
   244	SessionValues provide cross-agent key-value storage within a single run:
   245	
   246	```go
   247	// Set before running
   248	runner.Run(ctx, msgs, adk.WithSessionValues(map[string]any{"user": "Alice"}))
   249	
   250	// Access in Instruction via f-string
   251	Instruction: "The current user is {user}."
   252	```

1	AgentAsTool wraps an Agent as a Tool, enabling one agent to call another via function calling with isolated message history.
     2	
     3	## NewAgentTool
     4	
     5	```go
     6	import "github.com/cloudwego/eino/adk"
     7	
     8	agentTool := adk.NewAgentTool(ctx, subAgent, options...)
     9	```
    10	
    11	The wrapped agent:
    12	- Receives a fresh task description (not the parent's full history)
    13	- Shares SessionValues with the parent agent
    14	- By default does NOT emit internal AgentEvents to the parent's iterator
    15	
    16	To emit internal events, set `EmitInternalEvents: true` in the parent's ToolsConfig.
    17	
    18	## Basic Usage
    19	
    20	```go
    21	import (
    22	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/adk"
    23	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/components/tool"
    24	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/compose"
    25	)
    26	
    27	// Create a sub-agent
    28	researchAgent, _ := adk.NewChatModelAgent(ctx, &adk.ChatModelAgentConfig{
    29	    Name:        "Researcher",
    30	    Description: "Searches and summarizes information on any topic",
    31	    Instruction: "Research the given topic thoroughly and provide a summary.",
    32	    Model:       cm,
    33	    ToolsConfig: adk.ToolsConfig{
    34	        ToolsNodeConfig: compose.ToolsNodeConfig{
    35	            Tools: []tool.BaseTool{webSearchTool},
    36	        },
    37	    },
    38	})
    39	
    40	// Wrap as tool
    41	researchTool := adk.NewAgentTool(ctx, researchAgent)
    42	
    43	// Use in parent agent
    44	parentAgent, _ := adk.NewChatModelAgent(ctx, &adk.ChatModelAgentConfig{
    45	    Name:        "Coordinator",
    46	    Description: "Coordinates research and writing tasks",
    47	    Instruction: "Use the Researcher tool to gather information, then write a report.",
    48	    Model:       cm,
    49	    ToolsConfig: adk.ToolsConfig{
    50	        ToolsNodeConfig: compose.ToolsNodeConfig{
    51	            Tools: []tool.BaseTool{researchTool},
    52	        },
    53	        EmitInternalEvents: true,  // See sub-agent's events
    54	    },
    55	})
    56	```
    57	
    58	## When to Use AgentAsTool
    59	
    60	- The sub-agent needs only a clear task description, not the parent's full conversation history
    61	- You want the parent to maintain control flow (call sub-agent, get result, continue reasoning)
    62	- You need isolated context between parent and sub-agent (each has its own message history)
    63	- SessionValues are shared between parent and sub-agent for cross-cutting concerns
